title: Using || for default values discards valid 0, empty string, and false
rule: x || fallback evaluates the fallback for every falsy x, which includes 0, '', false, NaN and 0n — not just null/undefined. So opts.port || 8080 turns an explicit port 0 into 8080, count || 10 ignores a legitimate 0, and name || 'guest' drops an intentional empty string. When the only values you want to replace are 'missing' (null/undefined), use the nullish coalescing operator ?? instead, which short-circuits only on null and undefined and preserves all other falsy-but-valid values.
triggers: default value applied even though I passed 0
empty string replaced by default unexpectedly
